● **Huntington’s Disease (HD)** is a **rare genetic neurodegenerative disorder** caused by a **mutation in the** **HTT gene**, leading to abnormal huntingtin proteins that **destroy brain cells.** It affects voluntary **movement**, **cognition**, and **memory**, progressively worsening and usually fatal within 15–20 years of symptom onset. **It is not an autoimmune disorder.** **So, statement I is not correct.**
● Huntington’s Disease follows an **autosomal dominant inheritance** pattern. If a **parent** carries the **defective HTT gene**, **each child has a 50% chance**, not 75%, of inheriting the mutation and developing HD. **So, statement lI is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**What are Autoimmune disorders?**
● Autoimmune disorders are conditions where the **immune system mistakenly attacks** **the body’s own healthy cells and tissues**, thinking they are harmful. This leads to inflammation, pain, and organ damage.
● **Common examples include** rheumatoid arthritis (joints), type 1 diabetes (pancreas), multiple sclerosis (nerves), lupus (skin, kidneys, joints), and Hashimoto’s thyroiditis (thyroid gland), each affecting different parts of the body.
**Relevance:** The Union and State Governments have yet to include Huntington’s disease in the rare category under the National Policy for Rare Diseases, a demand raised by patients and caregivers in 2024.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the SMILE scheme: l. It is a Central Sector scheme under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment.
II. 	 It provides comprehensive rehabilitation for
transgenders and people engaged in begging.
III. 	It includes educational linkages for children
of rehabilitated persons.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **SMILE (Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise)** is a **Central Sector** Scheme of the **Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment.** It has **two sub-schemes:** rehabilitation of transgender persons and rehabilitation of persons engaged in begging. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The umbrella scheme explicitly covers **comprehensive rehabilitation** measures for both **transgender** persons and people engaged in **begging**, including healthcare, shelter, counselling, skill development, and livelihood opportunities. **So, statement Il is correct.**
● The scheme will target **all Indian citizens engaged in begging across the country**, especially in urban areas, municipal corporations, pilgrimage centres, tourist spots, and other public spaces.
● The scheme includes **educational linkages** for children of rehabilitated persons, ensuring their **enrolment** **in government-run schools** and providing **after-school tuition and homework support** for effective mainstreaming. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Government of India launched a 15 days Entrepreneurship Development Programme under the SMILE scheme.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With regard to the 3rd Generation Partnership
Project (3GPP), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is a global collaboration that develops
protocols for mobile telephony, including standards for 5G and 6G technologies.
II. 	 It works under the aegis of the International
Telecommunication Union (ITU), a specialized agency of the United Nations.
III. 	Telecommunications Standards Development
Society of India is the organizational partner of 3GPP from India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP)** was established in 1998 to develop 3G mobile standards.
○ Its primary function is to develop technical specifications for various mobile telephony standards, including the current 5G and ongoing work on 6G technologies.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● **3GPP** is **not an initiative of the International Telecommunication Union (ITU)**. It is an independent consortium of seven telecommunications standard development organizations (ARIB, ATIS, CCSA, ETSI, TSDSI, TTA, TTC), known as Organizational Partners from around the world. **While 3GPP** **submits its completed standards to the ITU for formal adoption, it operates autonomously**. **So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● The Indian Telecom Industry, comprising operators and manufacturers, Academia and R&D organizations came together to form **Telecommunications Standards Development Society, India (TSDSI)** in 2014. TSDSI is an autonomous, membership based, standards development organization (SDO) for Telecom/ICT products and services in India.
○ **TSDSI is the Organizational Partner of 3GPP.** This entitles TSDSI members to become Individual Members of 3GPP through TSDSI and contribute to 3GPP Specifications, taking their IPR into the global arena. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** In a historic first for India and the global telecom community, the 3GPP Radio Access Networks (RAN) Working Group meetings – RAN1 through RAN5 – were inaugurated on 25th August, in Whitefield, Bengaluru.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Tiger Reserve, Elephant Reserve, other species-specific reserves, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Sundarbans Tiger Reserve:
I. 	 It is the largest tiger reserve in India in terms
of area.
II. 	 It is the only mangrove ecosystem in the
world to harbour tigers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Sundarban Tiger Reserve** is located in the coastal districts of **West Bengal**, at the southernmost tip of the **Gangetic delta**, bordering the Bay of Bengal. It is also a **National Park** and **Biosphere Reserve.**
● After its **recent expansion in 2025**, Sundarbans Tiger Reserve covers 3,629.57 sq km, making it the **second-largest tiger reserve** in India. The **largest** remains **Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam** Tiger Reserve (3,727.82 sq km) in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.  **So, statement I is not correct.**
● Sundarbans is the **only mangrove ecosystem in the entire world that harbours tigers**, where the **Royal** **Bengal Tiger** has adapted to saline water, swimming, and unique mangrove conditions.  **So, statement II** **is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Sundarbans tiger reserve became India’s second-biggest tiger reserve after the standing committee of the National Board of Wildlife (NBWL) approved the West Bengal government’s proposal to increase its area.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about Vithalbhai Patel:
I. 	 He was the first Indian to become the President
of the Central Legislative Assembly.
II. 	 He opposed Mahatma Gandhi’s decision to
suspend the Non-Cooperation Movement.
III. 	He along with Motilal Nehru and Chittaranjan
Das founded the Swaraj Party in 1923.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● Vithalbhai Patel was born in the year 1873, on September 27. He was the older brother of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. He became a member of Bombay Legislative Council. He was elected to the Imperial Legislative Council in 1918.
● He served as the Mayor of Bombay Municipal Corporation from 1923 till 1925. **In 1924, he became a** **member of the Central Legislative Assembly** and rose to become **its first Indian President on August** **22, 1925**. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Vithalbhai **opposed Mahatma Gandhi’s decision to suspend the Non-Cooperation Movement** against the British after the **Chauri Chaura incident**. He along with leaders, including Subhas Chandra Bose, Motilal Nehru, Chittaranjan Das, among others, **founded the Swaraj Party in 1923**. **So, statements II** **and III are correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Other Contributions of Vithalbhai Patel:**
● He never truly accepted the philosophy and leadership of Mahatma Gandhi, but he joined the Congress in 1915 and when the Special Session of Congress was held in Bombay in August 1918, Vithalbhai was appointed Chairman of the Reception Committee.
● **In 1919 he went to England**, in connection with the Government of India Bill, as a member of the Congress delegation to present their case before the Joint Select Committee.
● When he was presiding over the session of the Central Legislative Assembly, on April 8, 1929, Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw two bombs and pamphlets in the Assembly.
● He **established an independent Parliament Secretariat**, with the officials in it reporting to none but the speaker.
● **In 1933** Vithalbhai and Bose had met in Vienna and issued a strongly worded joint statement, the **Patel-** **Bose manifesto**, calling for a new radical leadership of the Independence movement, in opposition of Gandhiji’s decision to suspend the civil disobedience movement.
**Relevance:** Recently, the Parliament celebrated the 100th of the first Indian president of the Central Legislative Council.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the India’s BioE3 Policy:
I. 	 It is an initiative by the Ministry of
Environment, Forest and Climate Change to promote a regenerative bioeconomy.
II. 	 The National Biofoundry Network is an
integral part of BioE3 Policy.
III. 	It focuses on India’s domestic biomanufacturing capabilities for precision biotherapeutics and smart proteins.
IV. 	 The goal of the policy aligns with the vision
of ‘Net-Zero’ carbon economy of the country.
Which of the statements given above is correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● **The Biotechnology for Employment, Economy & Environment (BioE3) Policy is an initiative of the** **Department of Biotechnology which falls under the Ministry of Science and Technology.** Its objective is to promote a regenerative, circular bioeconomy by developing sustainable, bio-based solutions. The Policy aims at revolutionizing the biomanufacturing process for enhanced efficiency, sustainability, and quality while also accelerating the development and production of bio-based high-value products. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● **Biofoundries** are specialized facilities designed to streamline the development of biological systems using high-throughput, automated technologies. They integrate various processes—such as DNA synthesis, gene editing, strain engineering, and metabolic pathway optimization—into a seamless workflow, enabling researchers to rapidly prototype and iterate biological constructs.
○ The **BioE3** **policy explicitly aims to establish a National Biofoundry Network** to accelerate the discovery, development, and scaling of new bio-products and biomanufacturing technologies. On August 27, 2025, the government launched the country's first National Biofoundry Network. **So,** **statement II is correct.**
● A key goal of the BioE3 Policy is to strengthen **India’s domestic biomanufacturing capabilities**. This includes developing and producing high-value biological products like **biotherapeutics** (e.g., cell therapies) and **smart proteins (e.g., lab-grown Insulin)**, thereby reducing the country's dependence on international supply chains and imports. **So, statement III is correct.**
● The **BioE3 Policy** is aligned with India’s vision of **Green Growth** (announced in the Union Budget 202324) and with the **‘Lifestyle for Environment (LiFE)’** mission, which envisions collective approach towards sustainability. The Policy also aligns with the **vision of ‘Net-Zero’ carbon economy** of the country. **So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Union Cabinet has approved the ‘BioE3 (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) Policy for ‘Fostering High Performance Biomanufacturing’.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Quick Reaction Surface to Air Missiles
II. 	 Very Short-Range Air Defence System
III. 	Laser-based Directed Energy Weapon
IV. 	 MALE Unmanned Aerial Vehicle
How many of the above are components of the Integrated Air Defence Weapon System of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has successfully conducted the first flight-tests of an Integrated Air Defence Weapon System (IADWS). The test was conducted off the coast of Odisha, in which three different targets were simultaneously destroyed at different ranges and altitudes.
● **The IADWS** is a multi-layered air defence system, which **includes three components** — Quick Reaction Surface to Air Missiles (QRSAM), the advanced **Very Short-Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS)** **missiles**, and a **high-power laser-based Directed Energy Weapon (DEW)**.
● While QRSAM has been designed and developed by the DRDO, VSHORADS and DEW have been developed by Research Centre Imarat (RCI) and Centre for High Energy Systems and Sciences (CHESS) respectively, both Hyderabad-based facilities of the DRDO.
o **QRSAM** has been designed and developed by the DRDO, is a short-range Surface to Air Missile system, primarily designed to provide a protective shield to moving armoured columns of the Army from enemy aerial attacks. It has a fully automated **360-degree search (two radars —** **Active Array Battery Surveillance Radar and Active Array Battery Multifunction Radar)** **and track capability** and can fire on short halts. The system has an operation range of three to 30
kilometers. The weapon system can neutralise targets between the range of 300 meters and six kilometers, including drones and other classes of aerial threats. **So, point I is correct.** o **VSHORADS** is a fourth generation, technically advanced miniaturised Man Portable Air Defence System (MANPAD). **So, point II is correct.** o A high-power **laser-based Directed Energy Weapon (DEW)** can defeat fixed wing Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) and swarm drones, cause structural damage and disable their surveillance sensors. DEW is said to have a range of less than three kilometres. **So, point III is correct.**
● IADWS is used to neutralise UAV and swarm drones, so Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) UAV is not a component of it. **So, point IV is not correct.** **So, only three points given above are correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** DRDO successfully conducted the maiden flight-tests of Integrated Air Defence Weapon System.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Hazardous, Radioactive, E-Waste, Heavy Metals, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements regard to the
selenium pollution:
I. 	 Selenium is a trace element that is beneficial
to the human body in small quantities but can become toxic in excess.
II. 	 There is no known safe level of exposure to
selenium.
III. 	Copper smelting activity is a source of
selenium pollution in India.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● **Selenium is an essential trace element** and essential for proper functioning of metabolism for example, **Selenoproteins** like glutathione peroxidases act as powerful antioxidants and being incorporated into **enzyme, iodothyronine deiodinases**, selenium plays also an essential role in the metabolism of thyroid hormones.
○ However, when selenium intake **exceeds the safe limit**, it can become toxic, leading to a condition known as **selenosis**. It may lead to **Kashin-Beck disease**, which results in joint and bone disease. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **permissible limit for selenium in drinking water varies by region**; for example, the **Bureau of** **Indian Standards (BIS) has set a permissible limit of 0.01 mg/L in India (Equivalent to 10 part per** **billion or ppb)**, while the **U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA**) has an enforceable standard (MCL) of **0.05 mg/L (or 50 parts per billion)**. The **World Health Organization (WHO) has a guideline** **value of 10 µg/L (or 0.01 mg/L). So, statement II is not correct.**
● **Copper smelting releases selenium into the environment** in the form of selenium dioxide and particulate matter. This happens because most copper ores are sulphide based and contain selenium as a trace element.  “Lead, arsenic, selenium and particulate matter are released from copper smelters. **So,** **statement III is correct.** **So, only two of the statements given above are correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge box**
**Central Ground Water Board and Central Ground Water Authority**
● **Central Ground Water Board (CGWB)** is the National Apex Agency entrusted with the responsibilities of providing scientific inputs for management, exploration, monitoring, assessment, augmentation and regulation of ground water resources of the country.
○ **Established in 1970**, CGWB was initially formed by renaming the Exploratory Tube Wells Organization and was later merged with the Ground Water Wing of the Geological Survey of India in 1972. ○ **CGWB is headed** by **a chairman and has six Members**.
● The Hon’ble Supreme Court of India passed several orders in 1996 issuing directions to the Government of India for setting up of **a Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA)**.
● Under **sub-section (3) of Section 3 of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986**, CGWA was constituted (January 1997) for the purposes of regulation and control of groundwater management and development.
○ CGWA is responsible for regulation and control, management and development of ground water in the country and issuing necessary regulatory directions for this purpose. ○ It grants No Objection Certificates (NOC) for extraction of ground water. ○ CGWA can also resort to penal provisions contained in the said Act. ○ The jurisdiction of the Authority extends to the whole of India.
**Relevance:** Recently,  high selenium levels in groundwater in parts of Haryana and Punjab, specifically in the Jhajjar and Rupnagar districts, were detected in a small number of samples exceeding the permissible limit.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
'Aadi Vaani', was recently launched for which one
of the following purposes?

options.a:
To promote traditional Indian music forms of
tribal communities
options.b:
To translate tribal languages through an
Artificial-Intelligence-based portal
options.c:
To sell tribal handicrafts via an online
platform
options.d:
To establish community radio networks for
remote tribal regions

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Adi Vaani is an AI-based translation tool** that serves as the foundation for a future large language model dedicated to tribal languages. The project combines advanced AI technologies with community-driven approaches to protect, promote, and revitalize tribal languages and cultures across India.
● It is **developed by a national consortium of premier institutions** led by IIT Delhi with BITS Pilani, IIIT Hyderabad, and IIIT Nava Raipurin collaboration withTribal Research Institutes (TRIs).
● It is launched with capabilities to translate the following Adivasi languages: Bhili (Bhil language), Mundari (Munda language), Gondi (Gond language), Santali, Kui (Kandha language), and Garo (Garo language). The app will be **able to translate speech and text in Hindi and English to these languages** **and vice versa**. **It aims to**:
○ Provide interactive language learning for students and early learners. ○ Preserve digitally, folklore, oral traditions, and cultural heritage. ○ Support digital literacy, healthcare communication, and civic inclusion in tribal communities. ○ Spread awareness of the government schemes and important speeches **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Ministry of Tribal Affairs has launched the beta version of “Adi Vaani,” India's first AI-powered translator for tribal languages.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Tiger Reserve, Elephant Reserve, other species-specific reserves, etc.
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Anamalai Tiger Reserve,
which one of the following statements is **not** correct?

options.a:
It is located on the southern side of the
Western Ghat landscape in Tamil Nadu.
options.b:
Synchronous lighting by mega congregations
of fireflies is observed here every year.
options.c:
It is a part of Agasthyamala Biosphere
Reserve.
options.d:
It hosts the world heritage sites of Kariyan
shola and Manjampatti.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● **Anamalai Tiger Reserve (ATR)** is located on the southern side of the **Western Ghat** landscape in **Tamil** **Nadu**. Earlier it was earlier known as Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park. It was declared as a Tiger reserve in the year 2007.
o Aliyar, Nirar, and Uppar rivers, which originate in the Anamalai Hills and flow through the reserve's valleys. o It is surrounded by Parambikulum Tiger Reserve on the East, Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and Eravikulum National Park on the South Western side.
● Anamalai Tiger Reserve (ATR) is **known** for the rare phenomenon of **synchronous lighting by mega** **congregations of fireflies every year**. This phenomenon, which occurs during the post-monsoon season, involves large groups of fireflies blinking in unison, creating a spectacular visual display.
o As per the study of Tamil Nadu Forest Department at least eight species of fireflies light up these forests with their bioluminescence.
o Through **DNA barcoding** method eight species of fireflies, namely **Abscondita perplexa,** **Abscondita terminalis, Asymmetricata humeralis, Curtos species (three different species),** **Lamprigera sp. and Pyrocoelia sp**, were identified by the Advanced Institute for Wildlife Conservation (AIWC), from the firefly larvae. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Anamalai Tiger Reserve is not a part of Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve.** Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve, located in the southern part of the Western Ghats in India and added to UNESCO’s list of ‘World Network of Biosphere Reserves’ in March 2016. Spreads across Kerala (Thiruvananthapuram & Kollam) and Tamil Nadu (Tirunelveli & Kanniyakumari). **Protected areas which fall under it are:**
o Periyar Tiger Reserve (Kerala). o Srivilliputhur Grizzled Squirrel Wildlife Sanctuary (TN). o Kalakkad-Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve (TN). o Neyyar, Peppara, and Shendurney Wildlife Sanctuaries. **So, statement III is correct.**
● The Kariyan shola, Grass hills and Manjampatti of Anamalai Tiger Reserve has been identified as a world heritage site by UNESCO. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**DNA Barcoding**
● DNA barcoding is a technique for identifying specimens by analysing small, standardised DNA segments. This "barcode" is a **specific gene sequence, which varies** **significantly between species** but remains consistent within them.
o For example, the cytochrome c oxidase subunit I (COI) gene is the standard DNA barcode for animals.
● DNA barcodes consist of a standardized short sequence of DNA, 400–800 base pairs.
● It is largely used for identifying species that are difficult to distinguish by appearance alone, such as insect larvae, plant fragments etc.
**Relevance:** With the increasing human-animal conflict deaths in estate areas, officials of the Anamalai Tiger Reserve (ATR) have started enumerating tigers and other carnivores, including the leopard population, outside the forest areas of Valparai and Manombolly.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
Project 17 Alpha (P17A):
I. 	 It was launched to build seven advanced
stealth frigates for the Indian Navy.
II. 	 Its ships have been designed in-house by
the Indian Navy's Warship Design Bureau (WDB).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● **Project 17 Alpha (P17A)** is launched for **building seven advanced stealth frigates for the Indian Navy**. The **Project 17 Alpha** follows the earlier P-17 Shivalik-class frigates and features a Combined Diesel or Gas (CODOG) propulsion system. While the first two frigates launched under this project are **INS Nilgiri** **and INS Himgiri**, the third one is named **INS Udaygiri**. INS Nilgiri and INS Himgiri, were launched in September 2019 and December 2020, respectively. INS Udaygiri was launched on May 17, 2022.
o INS Udaygiri and INS Himgiri were commissioned into the Indian Navy on August 26, 2025. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● The **Project 17A (P17A) frigates are being designed and built in India by the Navy's Warship Design** **Bureau (WDB)**. The project is a testament to India's growing self-reliance in naval shipbuilding and design, which is a major focus of the "Aatmanirbhar Bharat" (self-reliant India) initiative. **So, statement** **II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Project 17 and Project 17B**
● **Project 17** was conceived in the 1990s to meet the Indian Navy's need for a class of stealthy frigates that were to be designed and built in India. **The Shivalik class or Project 17** class is a class of multi-role frigates in service with the Indian Navy.
o They are the **first stealth warships** built in India. o A total of three ships were built between 2000 and 2010, and all three were in commission by 2012. o All ships of the class were built by Mazagon Dock Limited. The class and the lead vessel have been named for the Shivalik hills. o Shivalik is the first Indian naval vessel to use a combined diesel or gas (CODOG)
propulsion system.
● In September 2024, the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) has accorded an Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) to **Project 17 Bravo** of the Indian Navy, **under which seven new state-of-the-** **art Frigates would be built.**
o The Project 17 Bravo stealth frigates are a follow-on of Nilgiri class frigates, also known as Project-17A class frigates, currently being built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) and Garden Reach Shipbuilders and Engineers (GRSE).
**Relevance:** Indian Navy commissioned the latest state of the art Project 17A multi-mission stealth frigates Udaygiri and Himgiri at Naval Base, Visakhapatnam, on 26 Aug 2025.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With regard to New World screwworm, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 It is an insect, native to tropical and subtropical
America.
II. 	 It causes myiasis disease.
III. 	Its life cycle is independent of the host.
IV. 	 It has been eradicated from the United States
of America.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d** **Explanation**:
● **Cochliomyia hominivorax**, known as the New World screwworm, is a dipteran **native to tropical and** **subtropical America** that **causes myiasis** in warm-blooded animals and poses a public health concern.
o The parasites are named after the screwlike way they burrow into the tissue. Their Latin name, **Cochliomyia hominivorax**, “literally means man-eater.” Due to its impact, the FAO and WHO have classified myiasis caused by **C. hominivorax** as a priority disease for eradication in the Americas. **So, statements I and II are correct.**
● **According to the World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH)**, the New World screwworm has been **successfully eradicated from the United States and large parts of Central America through the** **sterile insect technique (SIT) program between 1972 and 1991**.
o However, recently, new cases of New World screwworm infestation have been reported in Panama, Costa Rica, Nicaragua and Honduras. **So, statement IV is correct.**
● The **life cycle of the New World screwworm is dependent on a host.** The female fly is attracted to and lays eggs on an open wound or mucous membrane of a living, warm-blooded animal. **So, statement III** **is not correct.**
o The species goes through **three larval stages** **within its host** before completing metamorphosis in the soil, emerging as an adult fly after a pupation period of up to 240 hours. o New World screwworm **larvae specifically feed on living tissue of warm-blooded animals**,
which can be fatal if untreated. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH)**
● The World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH), founded in 1924 as the Office International des Epizooties (OIE), is an intergovernmental organization dedicated to enhancing animal health and welfare globally.
● With 183 member countries, including India, WOAH establishes international standards through the **Terrestrial Animal Health Code (TAHC)** which provides science-based guidelines on disease surveillance, diagnostics, biosecurity, vaccination, and the safe trade of animals and animal products.
● WOAH membership does not provide any financial incentives or subsidies for private companies.
● Notably, India's FMD Control Programme has received WOAH endorsement, and India's selfdeclaration of 32 HPAI-free poultry compartments has also been approved.
● WOAH designates specialized institutions as WOAH Reference Laboratories, with India hosting such laboratories for HPAI, Rabies, Leptospirosis, PPR, and Equine Piroplasmosis.
**Relevance**: The New World Screwworm (NWS), a flesh-eating fly that is rapidly spreading across parts of the United States, has raised global alarm over its potential impact on livestock, food security, and rural economies.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
With regard to the National Designated Authority
(NDA), which of the following statements is/are correct?
I. 	 It has been established to implement the Paris
Agreement.
II. 	 It is a 21 member committee headed by the
Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a** **Explanation**:
● The Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has constituted the **National** **Designated Authority (NDA)** to implement Article 6 of the Paris Agreement and establish mechanisms for carbon markets. **So, statement I is correct**
o Article 6 was finally passed by countries at the 29th edition of the climate COP in Baku, Azerbaijan, in November 2024. o **The creation of an NDA is a mandatory requirement under Article 6.**
● **It is a 21-member committee** **headed by the Secretary of the Environment Ministry**. Representatives include officials from the Ministry of External Affairs, Ministry of Renewable Energy, NITI Aayog and Steel Ministry among others. The maximum representation is from the Environment Ministry. **So,** **statement II is not correct**
● **The NDA’s responsibilities include** recommending to the Union government the list of activities that can be considered for the trading of emission reduction units from projects under Article 6, modifying them from time to time keeping in view national sustainable goals,
o country-specific criteria and other national priorities; o receive projects or activities for evaluation, approval and authorisation; o authorise the use of emission reduction units from projects for use towards achievement of Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC). **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Carbon Market under the Paris Agreement**
● **Article 6** of the Paris Agreement sets out how countries can pursue voluntary cooperation to reach their climate targets.
o It enables international cooperation to tackle climate change and unlock financial support
for developing countries.
● **Article 6.2** sets up a carbon market which allows countries to sell any extra emission reductions they have achieved compared to their target.
o These credits would be called Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes (**ITMOs**).
● **Article 6.4** (Sometimes called the **Sustainable Development Mechanism, or SDM**), resembles much more the Clean Development Mechanism, except that it will not be restricted to projects implemented in developing countries.
o Under this market, it is expected that project developers will reduce emissions through
specific actions in a country and sell these emission reductions to another country/company/person. o A designated national authority (DNA) is the organization granted responsibility by a
Party to authorize and approve participation in Article 6.4 projects. o Establishment of a DNA is one of the requirements for participation by a Party in the
Article 6.4 mechanism. o As of 5 August 2025, 113 countries have submitted a designated national authority.
● **Article 6.8 of the Paris Agreement establishes a framework for non-market approaches to** **climate action**, encouraging voluntary cooperation between countries and other stakeholders to achieve their Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) through non-market means such as capacity-building, finance, and technology transfer.
**Relevance:** The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has announced a ‘National Designated Authority (NDA),’ a mandatory requirement under the provisions of the 2015 Paris Agreement to enable a carbon emissions trading regime.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Security and Strategic Issues (Terrorism, Cyber, Maritime, Exercises, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Military Exercise Chief Participants
I. - Super Garuda Shield
India and Saudi Arabia
II. - Exercise Maitree India and Egypt
III. - Exercise BRIGHT STAR
India and Thailand
IV. - SLINEX India and Sri Lanka
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Super Garuda Shield 2025** started in late August in Jakarta, marking the largest iteration of the **defence** **exercise between Indonesia-United States**. It is for demonstration of Indo-Pacific solidarity. Featuring 6,500 troops from more than a dozen countries, including Australia, India and Japan. **So, pair I is not** **correctly matched.**
● The **14th edition of the India** - **Thailand joint military** **Exercise MAITREE-XIV** commenced with a grand opening ceremony at Joint Training Node (JTN), Umroi, Meghalaya. Exercise MAITREE, instituted in 2006. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● **Exercise Bright Star** is **a multilateral exercise hosted by Egypt in conjunction with the US since 1980** and is amongst the largest Tri-Service multilateral exercises in the region. The exercise is held biennially. In 2025, more than 700 Indian personnel from the Armed Forces & Headquarters Integrated Defence Staff joined the Multilateral Exercise ‘BRIGHT STAR 2025. **So, pair III is not correctly matched.**
● **SLINEX** is a biennial bilateral naval exercise between Sri Lanka and India. **So, pair IV is correctly** **matched.** **So, one of the pairs given above is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** In the month of August, Indian armed forces conducted a series of bilateral military exercises with various countries.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
What is the 'Inverted duty structure' in the context
of Indian taxation?

options.a:
It is the tax paid on purchases for the business
which can be deducted on output tax.
options.b:
It arises when the tax rate levied on
intermediate inputs and raw materials exceed the tax rate on the final product.
options.c:
It is the liability to pay tax on the recipient of
supply of certain goods or services instead of the supplier of such goods or services.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: c** **Explanation**:
● An **inverted duty structure** is a situation where the GST rate on a finished product is lower than the GST rate on the raw materials or inputs used to manufacture that product. This creates a scenario where a manufacturer pays more tax on their inputs than they collect on their final sales.
o **For example**: Before September 2025, Man-made fibers and yarn were taxed at 18% and 12% GST, respectively whereas the final garment was taxed at a lower rate, such as 5%. o **However now** in September 2025, the GST rates on man-made fibers and yarns were reduced to 5% to correct this imbalance. **So, option (c) is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Meaning of Reverse Charge mechanism and Input Tax Credit:**
● **Reverse Charge means** the liability to pay tax is on the recipient of supply of goods or services instead of the supplier of such goods or services in respect of notified categories of supply. **There are two types of reverse charge scenarios provided in law.**
o First is dependent on the nature of supply and/or nature of supplier. o The second scenario is where taxable supplies by any unregistered person to a registered person is covered.
● **Input Tax Credit (ITC)** refers to the tax paid on purchases for the business which can be claimed as deduction at the time of paying tax on output tax.
**Relevance:** Inverted duty structure in India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) was addressed primarily through the 56th GST Council meeting, which focused on correcting it for textiles and fertilisers.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Critical and Strategic Minerals (Lithium, Cobalt, Titanium, KABIL, REEs, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With regard to the Rare Earth Magnets (REM),
consider the following statements:
I. 	 They are stronger than conventional magnets
due to their high magnetic energy density.
II. 	 Neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB) magnets
are the most common type of rare-earth magnet.
III. 	They are brittle and vulnerable to corrosion.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● **Rare earth magnets** are powerful permanent magnets made from rare earth elements. They tend to retain their properties almost permanently, and they are far lighter than traditional magnets made from iron. **They are significantly stronger than conventional magnets** due to their high magnetic energy density. They have superior resistance to demagnetization and can maintain their magnetic properties over extended periods.
o Their **high magnetic strength-to-weight ratio** makes them ideal for applications where efficiency and compactness are essential, such as in the motors of electric and hybrid vehicles, generators in wind turbines. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Neodymium-iron-boron (NdFeB)** magnets are the most common type of rare-earth magnet. **Dysprosium** is often added to the alloy to improve the magnet's resistance to demagnetization at high temperatures,
which is crucial for applications like electric vehicle motors. **Praseodymium** is also a component, often used in conjunction with neodymium. **So, statement II is correct.**
● These magnets tend to be **brittle and are vulnerable** to **corrosion**. Manufacturers typically nickel-plate the components of **rare earth magnets** to protect it from oxidation. **So, statement III is correct.**
● **Currently, IREL (India) Ltd, a PSU under the Department of Atomic Energy,** refines rare earth oxides from heavy metal ores, containing REEs like cerium, lanthanum, neodymium, and praseodymium.
However, production is limited. Moreover, the processing of refining oxides into alloys—and then into magnets—is highly technical and virtually non-existent in India. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India's electric two-wheeler industry, including market leader TVS Motor, is facing a severe and ongoing shortage of rare-earth magnets (REM).

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With regard to the National Awards to Teachers
(NAT), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is given to teachers from School,
Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) and Polytechnics.
II. 	 It is conferred by the Ministry of Education.
III. 	The parameters of evaluation give maximum
weightage to research publications.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The National Awards to Teachers were originally meant only for school teachers. However, following the reforms under the **National Education Policy (NEP) 2020,** the scope of the awards was broadened in 2023 to include exemplary faculty **from Higher Educational Institutions and Polytechnics as well.** This was done to ensure that outstanding teachers at all levels of the education system received recognition, in line with NEP’s emphasis on incentivising quality teaching and cultivating a culture of excellence. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The Ministry of Education, through its Department of Higher Education**, confers these awards as part of the ongoing national celebration of Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav. The official press release explicitly ties NAT-2024 to this theme, highlighting how the awards serve not just as recognition for individual teachers but also as part of the broader narrative of India’s educational and national development journey. **So, statement II is correct.**
● While parameters such as research and innovation, sponsored projects, consultancy, and faculty development activities are certainly considered, **the maximum weightage in the evaluation is given to** **teaching-learning effectiveness and outreach activities**. The intent of the award is to recognise teachers who not only excel in academics but also inspire and influence students and the wider community, making teaching and outreach the most heavily weighted components. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Organiser**: Ministry of Education (Department of Higher Education).
● Categories of Awards: ○ Category I: Teachers of Higher Educational Institutions (Engineering & Technology, Sciences, Humanities, Commerce, Management, Law, etc.) ○ Category II: Teachers of Polytechnics.
● **Evaluation Parameters:** Teaching-learning effectiveness, outreach activities, research and innovation, consultancy, and sponsored projects.
● **Nominations**: Invited via the Rashtriya Puraskar Portal with self, institutional, and peer nominations, encouraging Jan Bhagidari.
● NAT 2024 Awardees: 16 teachers were selected from IITs, IISER, BHU, State Universities, Polytechnics, and other HEIs across India.
**Relevance:** Department of Higher Education selected 16 teachers from HEIs and Polytechnics for National Awards to Teachers (NAT) 2024.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 The Tawi River flows through the city of
Jammu.
II. 	 The Sutlej River originates near Manali in
Himachal Pradesh.
III. 	The Pong Dam is built on the Beas River in
Himachal Pradesh.
IV. 	 The Beas River joins the Sutlej River in
Punjab.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Tawi River is a vital tributary of the Chenab River and flows prominently through the city of** **Jammu,** often described as its lifeline. Beyond its utilitarian role, the river holds religious and cultural significance for the local population and has long been considered sacred, referred to as “Suryaputri Tawi.” **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **Sutlej River** does not originate in Himachal Pradesh near Manali. It originates **in Tibet, from Lake** **Rakshastal, close to Mount Kailash.** The river flows westward, enters India through the Shipki La pass in Himachal Pradesh, and then traverses Punjab before entering Pakistan. Hence, while the Sutlej runs through Himachal Pradesh, it does not originate within Indian territory. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● **The Pong Dam**, also called **Maharana Pratap Sagar**, is built across the **Beas River in the Kangra** **district of Himachal Pradesh**. It is among the highest earth-fill dams in India and serves multiple purposes such as **irrigation, hydropower generation, and flood control.** The large reservoir created by the dam has been designated as a Ramsar site, hosting migratory birds and adding ecological as well as tourism value to the region. **So, statement III is correct.**
● The Beas River flows **south-west through Himachal Pradesh into Punjab,** where it eventually **merges** **with the Sutlej River.** This confluence forms part of the Indus river system and plays a crucial role in supporting agriculture in Punjab and adjoining regions. Historically, the Beas marked the easternmost point of Alexander’s advance in India, further adding to its significance.**So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Bhakra Beas Management Board (BBMB) reported a record-breaking monsoon season for its key reservoirs, with the Pong Dam receiving its highest-ever inflows since its inception.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With regard to the Prime Minister Street Vendor’s
AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi) Scheme, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It provides working capital loans to street
vendors.
II. 	 The beneficiaries who repay their second
loan can access a UPI-linked RuPay Credit Card and are eligible for digital cashback incentives up to ₹1,600.
III. 	It is jointly implemented by the Ministry of
Housing & Urban Affairs and Department of Financial Services.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The PM SVANidhi Scheme, launched in June 2020**, was a landmark initiative to **provide working** **capital loans to street vendors during the COVID-19 pandemic.** With its restructuring in 2025, the scheme has been extended and strengthened to promote credit access, digital empowerment, and holistic socio-economic development, ensuring sustained livelihood security for street vendors. **So, statement I** **is correct.**
● A major innovation in the scheme is the introduction of a UPI-linked RuPay Credit Card for beneficiaries who successfully repay their second loan. This card **provides immediate access to credit and is** **accompanied by cashback incentives up to ₹1,600** for retail and wholesale transactions, thereby encouraging digital adoption among street vendors. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The scheme is jointly implemented by the Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs **(MoHUA) and the** **Department of Financial Services (DFS**), with DFS facilitating credit access through banks and financial institutions. It also strengthens the ‘SVANidhi se Samriddhi’ initiative, which integrates multiple government schemes through profiling and outreach at the grassroots, ensuring that street vendors and their families receive comprehensive welfare benefits. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Union Cabinet approved the restructuring and extension of the PM SVANidhi Scheme.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With regard to the Unified District Information
System for Education Plus (UDISE+), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is an educational management information
system by the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
II. 	 It enables recognised schools to record and
submit real-time data on school profile, students, and teachers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
UDISE+ is India’s core school-education data backbone, capturing granular information from every recognised school each academic year. By standardising inputs across infrastructure, students and teachers, it supports evidence-based planning and targeted interventions.
● UDISE+ operates under the Department of School Education & Literacy under the **Ministry of** **Education**, as a centralised EMIS through which recognised schools submit data in real time. It covers three integrated streams: **school profile & facilities, student module, and teacher profile, ensuring** **complete visibility into infrastructure, access, learning cohorts, and human resources.** The system is designed for seamless school-level entry and multi-tier validation by designated officials at block, district, state and national levels. **So, statement I is not correct and statement II is correct.**
● The UDISE+ 2024–25 report notes that the total number of teachers has crossed the **1-crore mark, a** **headline milestone since UDISE+ began**. This figure underscores both the size of India’s schooling ecosystem and the utility of UDISE+ in tracking workforce trends for policy and budgeting.
**Knowledge Box**
● **Unique IDs:** Every onboarded school gets an 11-digit UDISE code and login credentials to enable data entry and updates.
● **Modules**: ○ School profile & facilities (infrastructure, services) ○ Student module (general/academic profile via Permanent Education Number, activities) ○ Teacher profile (general, academic, and appointment details of teaching & nonteaching staff)
● Governance & Quality: Data is validated at block, district, state, and national levels; the School User Directory module manages authorised users across tiers.
● Purpose: Central platform for storage, updating, analysis, and sharing of school-education data—foundation for planning, monitoring, and targeted schemes.
**Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Ministry of Education released a report on Unified District Information System for Education Plus (UDISE+) 2024-25 on school education of India.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: National Park, Wildlife Sanctuary, Biosphere Reserve
questions.questionText:
With regard to Gorumara National Park, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 It is located in the Dooars–Terai of West
Bengal and is renowned for riverine grasslands and sal forests.
II. 	 It has the highest population of one-horned
rhinoceros in the country.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Gorumara National Park** is a compact but biodiverse protected area at the Himalayan foothills in West Bengal. It is renowned for **riverine grasslands and sal forests,** and for being a stronghold of the greater one-horned rhinoceros in the state.
● **Gorumara National Park is located in the Dooars–Terai belt of Jalpaiguri district in West Bengal.** It lies along the banks of the Murti and Raidak rivers, which enrich its ecosystem. This location makes it a biodiversity hotspot with moist deciduous forests and grasslands.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● **While Gorumara is well known for its population of the Indian one-horned rhinoceros, it does not** **host the highest number in the country.** The park has a relatively small but protected rhino population. **The highest concentration of these rhinos is found in Kaziranga National Park in Assam**. Key fauna includes Greater one-horned rhino, Asian elephant, gaur (bison), leopard; notable birdlife. **So, statement** **II is not correct.**
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The population of one-horned rhinos has risen at the Gorumara National Park in Jalpaiguri with the birth of two rhinos in mid-August.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
With regard to the Glanders, consider the following
statements:
I. 	 It is a highly contagious disease affecting
horses, mules and donkeys.
II. 	 It is caused by the virus Burkholderia mallei.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Glanders** is a **highly contagious and often fatal disease affecting horses, mules, and donkeys,** with **zoonotic** potential to infect humans. To strengthen India’s preparedness, the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying recently issued a Revised National Action Plan that modifies containment zones, enhances surveillance, and integrates a One Health approach for eradication. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Glanders is caused by the **bacterium (not virus) Burkholderia mallei** and primarily impacts equines such as horses, mules, and donkeys. Given its severity and zoonotic potential, **it is officially a notifiable** **disease under the PCICDA Act, 2009.** This legal provision requires mandatory reporting of any suspected or confirmed cases to authorities, enabling swift containment measures and safeguarding public as well as animal health.  **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The **Revised National Action Plan** has explicitly reduced the infected zone radius from 5 km to 2 km and redefined the surveillance area as 2–10 km instead of the earlier 5–25 km. This recalibration was intended to bring more precision and feasibility in monitoring, containment, and movement restrictions, while aligning with improved diagnostic capabilities and rapid response mechanisms. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying (DAHD) under the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying has released a revised National Action Plan on Glanders.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.questionText:
What are Salina aurantiamaculata and Salina
pseudomontana, recently seen in the news?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) has made a significant scientific breakthrough by discovering two new species of springtails — Salina aurantiamaculata and Salina pseudomontana in West Bengal. **These tiny soil** **arthropods, often overlooked, are critical to soil ecology and environmental monitoring,** making the finding an important milestone in biodiversity research.
● **The ZSI’s Apterygota section in Kolkata,** identified these two new species in 2025. Remarkably, this marks the first discovery of a Salina species in India after more than four decades, with the last one being recorded in 1979. With these additions, India’s tally of Salina species now stands at 17. This long gap highlights both the rarity of such discoveries and the importance of sustained taxonomic research in soil ecosystems.
● Both Salina aurantiamaculata and Salina pseudomontana belong to the group Collembola (springtails), **tiny soil-dwelling arthropods.** Despite being only a few millimetres long, they perform vital ecological functions such as breaking down organic matter, recycling nutrients, and improving soil structure. Collembola are considered **“ecosystem engineers**” and are also used as bioindicators to monitor soil health, pollution, and climate change impacts. Thus, their discovery adds both scientific and ecological value. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Unique features:** ○ Salina aurantiamaculata is named for its bright orange markings, a rare pigment pattern. ○ Salina pseudomontana resembles Salina montana but differs in the arrangement of bristles and the structure of the furcula (jumping appendage).
● **Ecological role:** Springtails enhance nutrient cycling, soil fertility, and ecosystem health.
● Evolutionary note: Springtails are among the oldest land-dwelling hexapods, dating back hundreds of millions of years.
● Research significance: The findings were published in the taxonomic journal Zootaxa (2025).
**Relevance:** After 4 decades, Zoological Survey of India records discovery of 2 new springtail species in West Bengal.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
With regard to the recent study about Gangotri
Glacier, consider the following statements:
I. 	 The Gangotri Glacier has lost about 10% of
its snowmelt flow due to rising temperatures and reduced snowfall.
II. 	 The reduced winter precipitation is one of the
major factors for the change in the regime of the glacier.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**The Gangotri Glacier, one of the largest Himalayan glaciers and the source of the Ganga**, has been under close scientific scrutiny due to its ecological and hydrological significance. Recent studies combining satellite and field data over four decades reveal clear climate change impacts, including reduced snowmelt, earlier peak flows, and changing runoff composition.
● A joint study by IIT Indore, ICIMOD, and US collaborators confirms that the **Gangotri Glacier lost** **around 10% of its snowmelt flow between 1980 and 2020.** Rising mean temperatures have curtailed snow formation, reducing snowmelt contributions to the river system. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Analysis shows **a shift in peak discharge timing from August to July.** This change is attributed to **reduced winter precipitation and earlier summer melting,** reflecting a climate-driven alteration in the glacier’s flow regime. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Despite the decline, **snowmelt remains the dominant contributor,** averaging 64% of the annual flow over the last four decades. Glacier melt contributes about 21%, rainfall-runoff 11%, and base flow 4%. The proportion of snowmelt has slipped from 73% in 1980–90 to about 63% in 2010–20.
● **The maximum decadal discharge was recorded in 2001–2010 (28.9 m³/s), corresponding with the** **highest decadal mean temperature (3.4°C).** The subsequent decade (2010–2020) did not surpass this record, though variability in snowmelt proportion was noted due to increased winter precipitation and slightly reduced temperatures. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● Length & Width: ~30 km long, ~4 km wide.
● Altitude: Snout at Gaumukh, ~4,000 m in Uttarkashi, Uttarakhand.
● Flow composition (1980–2020): Snowmelt 64%, Glacier melt 21%, Rainfall 11%, Base flow 4%.
● Temperature trend: Rise of ~0.5°C in 2001–2020 compared to 1980–2000.
● Implications: Impacts irrigation, hydropower, and water security in northern India.
● Broader context: Himalayan glaciers are thinning at ~46 cm/year on average.
**Relevance:** A study by IIT Indore and international collaborators reveals that the Gangotri Glacier has lost 10% of its snowmelt flow over four decades due to climate change.
